html:
See also: Other Set Pieces

Skeletons are the remains of other unknown adventurers who perished before the main character entered the world. They can occasionally be found in the wilderness, usually around Set Pieces, and often have random loot beside them. Skeletons can be struck with a Hammer to produce 2 Bone Shards.

Only 100 skeletons can exist in the world at one time, and any new ones generated after will vanish and leave ash behind.

When the player dies, an identical skeleton with a special character examination quote will be left in that location with the player's items, implying that all set pieces are dead players. When examining their own skeleton, characters will say the following:

DefaultFemaleMaleRobot

Shallow Grave

Shallow Grave replaces the Skeleton on the Wegame edition of Don't Starve Together, its function is exactly same as the Skeleton.

Crispy Skeleton

Exclusive to:

Crispy Skeleton is a variation of the skeleton found in the Dragonfly Nest in Don't Starve Together.

Pig and Merm Skeletons

Exclusive to:

Pig and Merm Skeletons can be found only in Caves in two copies. They always appear along with several Silks, under a beam of light. The backstory of these skeletons is shown in the short film Tales From the Constant: Falling.

Trivia

  • The model of the Skeleton is the same model as the unimplemented character Wilton found in the Don't Starve files.
  • The word 'Boon' means 'Favor' and given that skeletons make up the majority of boons, it's likely that this is a pun on the word "Bones".
  • There is a type of skeleton in Don't Starve Together that appears to have a Spear stuck in it, though when hammered, it does not drop a Spear.
  • Before the Quality of Life update, skeletons couldn't be struck with a Hammer to produce 2 Bone Shards in the base game.
  • Pig and Merm Skeletons were introduced in the Scrappy Scavengers update.

Gallery

  • A fallen Skeleton.
  • A dramatic Skeleton.
  • A sunken Skeleton.
  • A diving Skeleton.
  • A crawling Skeleton.
  • Warly standing next to 3 Skeletons.
  • Wormwood standing next to the Pigman and Merm skeletons.
  • Merm and Pigman Skeleton concept art from Scrappy Scavengers Annonce from Rhymes with Play.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

“Better you than me.”
–Wilson
“I hope you at least went out in a blaze of glory.”
–Willow
“Not enough muscle.”
–Wolfgang
“I envy your escape.”
–Wendy
“HAH, IT DIED. CLASSIC HUMAN MISTAKE.”
–WX-78
“An incredibly well preserved human skeleton.”
–Wickerbottom
“Sorry, friend.”
–Woodie
“Ha! I remember that one.”
–Maxwell
“Ah. I have seen quite a few of those in my time.”
–Wagstaff
“Rest easy in Valhalla.”
–Wigfrid
“Hello? Are you alright?”
–Webber
“Surfing the beyond...”
–Walani
“I have a bone to pick with you.”
–Warly
“Yer luck ran out, matey.”
–Woodlegs
“DEAD UNPIG!”
–Wilba
“Still alive?”
–Wormwood
“Always a good sign there's adventure ahead.”
–Wheeler
“A workplace safety reminder.”
–Winona
“The soul is long gone.”
–Wortox
“It dead.”
–Wurt
“Is it a real skeleton?”
–Walter
“This one's beyond my power to save...”
–Wanda
Skeletons are the remains of other unknown adventurers who perished before the main character entered the world. They can occasionally be found in the wilderness, usually around Set Pieces, and often have random loot beside them. Skeletons can be struck with a Hammer to produce 2 Bone Shards.
Only 100 skeletons can exist in the world at one time, and any new ones generated after will vanish and leave ash behind.

“%s was felled by %s. Live ön in Valhalla.”

“Ewwwwwwwwwww!”
–Willow
“Wolfgang does not like this!!”
–Wolfgang
“The flame has cleansed this poor wretched soul.”
–Wendy
“THE FLESHLING FRIED ITS CIRCUITS”
–WX-78
“You don't inspire a great deal of confidence, dear.”
–Wickerbottom
“Yikes.”
–Woodie
“At least the fire cut down on the smell.”
–Maxwell
“Rest easy, spirit. Yöur fight is över.”
–Wigfrid
“They're probably fine.”
–Webber
“A kitchen mishap, maybe?”
–Warly
“Oh. Oh no”
–Wormwood
“Yikes. Not a good way to go.”
–Winona
“Someone had a whoopsie.”

–Wurt
“I think the time for first aid has long passed.”
–Walter

–Wanda
Crispy Skeleton is a variation of the skeleton found in the Dragonfly Nest in Don't Starve Together.
Pig and Merm Skeletons[edit | edit source]
Exclusive to:

"skeleton_notplayer"
“These are not human bones.”
–Wilson
“Nasty! Who left their smelly old skeleton here?”
–Willow
“Should have eaten more protein.”
–Wolfgang
“All living things share the same fate. Thankfully.”
–Wendy
“A MEATLING WITH NO MEAT. COMICAL”
–WX-78
“Looking at the bone structure, this was clearly not human.”
–Wickerbottom
“Well, it's lookin' pretty dead.”
–Woodie
“You're not of much use to me now, are you?”
–Maxwell
“Didst thöu fall in battle, beast?”
–Wigfrid
“It makes us a little sad.”
–Webber
“I've made soup with worse things.”
–Warly
“Sleeping?”
–Wormwood
“Rest in peace, whatever you were.”
–Winona
“Love to stay and chew the fat, but you seem lacking of that.”
–Wortox
“Glorpt. Can't tell if Mermfolk or Pigfolk...”
–Wurt
“Think it's a monster skeleton, Woby?”
–Walter
“These old bones should remind us not to tarry.”
–Wanda
Pig and Merm Skeletons can be found only in Caves in two copies. They always appear along with several Silks, under a beam of light. The backstory of these skeletons is shown in the short film Tales From the Constant: Falling.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The model of the Skeleton is the same model as the unimplemented character Wilton found in the Don't Starve files.
The word 'Boon' means 'Favor' and given that skeletons make up the majority of boons, it's likely that this is a pun on the word "Bones".
There is a type of skeleton in Don't Starve Together that appears to have a Spear stuck in it, though when hammered, it does not drop a Spear.
Before the Quality of Life update, skeletons couldn't be struck with a Hammer to produce 2 Bone Shards in the base game.
Pig and Merm Skeletons were introduced in the Scrappy Scavengers update.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Bộ xương là hài cốt của những nhà thám hiểm vô danh khác đã thiệt mạng trước khi nhân vật chính bước vào thế giới. Đôi khi chúng có thể được tìm thấy ở nơi hoang dã, thường là xung quanh Set Pieces và thường có chiến lợi phẩm ngẫu nhiên bên cạnh. Bộ xương có thể bị đập bằng Búa để tạo ra 2 Mảnh Xương.

The backstory of these skeletons is shown in the short film ''[[Animated Shorts and Trailers#Tales From the Constant: Falling|Tales From the Constant: Falling]]''. {{Clear}} =={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * The model of the Skeleton is the same model as the unimplemented character [[Unimplemented Characters#Wilton|Wilton]] found in the ''[[Don't Starve]]'' files. * The word 'Boon' means 'Favor' and given that skeletons make up the majority of boons, it's likely that this is a pun on the word "Bones". * There is a type of skeleton in ''Don't Starve Together'' that appears to have a [[Spear]] stuck in it, though when hammered, it does not drop a [[Spear]]. * Before the ''[[Don't Starve/Version History|Quality of Life]]'' update, skeletons couldn't be struck with a Hammer to produce 2 Bone Shards in the base game. * Pig and Merm Skeletons were introduced in the ''[[From Beyond#Scrappy Scavengers|Scrappy Scavengers]]'' update. =={{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery== File:Skeleton lying.png|A fallen Skeleton.
